title: Thiruchuzhial
tagline: Thiruchuzhial — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Thirumeninathar temple (also called Boominathar temple or Tiruchuli temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Tiruchuli in Virudhunagar district, in the So
overview.description: Thirumeninathar temple (also called Boominathar temple or Tiruchuli temple) is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Tiruchuli in Virudhunagar district, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Thirumeninathar, and is represented by the lingam. His consort Parvati is depicted as Thunaimalaiyammai Amman. The temple is located on the Virudhunagar–Manamadurai road.

Manamadurai is a Town in Sivaganga district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, Situated 58 kilometres away from the heart of Madurai on the Cochin - Dhanushkodi National Highway. It is the 4th Largest Town in the Sivaganga district followed by Karaikudi, Sivaganga and Devakottai.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thiruchuzhial?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thiruchuzhial is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (45 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thiruchuzhial?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thiruchuzhial at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thiruchuzhial?
faq.a: Stays in Thiruchuzhial start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thiruchuzhial?
faq.a: The top attractions are Manamadurai taluk, Pudukulam, Idaikattur, Manamadurai.
